steps.title: Identify what the task requires
steps.content:
The student wants to emphasize a difference between the behavior of jellyfish and black dragonfish. The answer must mention both animals and explicitly contrast what each does with its light.
  • "Both jellyfish and black dragonfish are organisms that emit light, which is produced by chemical reactions in these organisms' cells.": Emphasizes a similarity (both emit light via chemical reactions), not a difference in behavior. ✗
  • "Black dragonfish emit a steady red light, which helps them locate prey in deep waters.": Describes only black dragonfish; jellyfish are absent, so no contrast between the two is possible. ✗
  • "Bioluminescence, the emission of light by living organisms, results from chemical reactions in organisms' cells.": Defines bioluminescence generally; neither animal is mentioned, let alone contrasted. ✗
  • "Jellyfish emit light to startle predators, whereas black dragonfish do so to locate prey.": Uses "whereas" to directly contrast the two animals' different purposes for emitting light, satisfying the student's goal. ✓

steps.title: Confirm the contrast
steps.content: Jellyfish emit light to startle predators, whereas black dragonfish do so to locate prey. names both organisms and explicitly contrasts their behavioral purposes (defense vs. predation) with the contrast word "whereas." No other choice does both. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
